description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline for a Rust project using cargo with two stages: build and release, caching Cargo.lock and the target/ and $CARGO_HOME directories (key id002, pull for release-build and pull‑push for debug-build and nightly-test) and defining branch‑only rules and regex release/hotfix rules. In the build stage add check-test (runs cargo check --all-targets --all-features --bins --tests --benches --examples --color always, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features --color always -- -Dclippy::all, then ./fix.sh), audit-test (runs cargo audit --deny warnings --color always), debug-build (artifacts target/debug/samotop-server and Cargo.lock, runs cargo build --all-targets --all-features --color always, cargo test --all-targets --all-features --color always, ./clean.sh), nightly-test (uses rustup default nightly, runs cargo test, cargo clippy --all-features, cargo check, ./clean.sh with a nightly‑prefixed cache), example-cmd, example-default, example-dir, example-lmtp (adds a dovecot/dovecot:2.3.11 service) and example-prudence (allow_failure, custom netcat diff checks) all extending .example-template which runs rustc --version, cargo --version, cargo build --example ${EXAMPLE:?}, then RUST_LOG=trace cargo run --example ${EXAMPLE:?} &, sleeps, netcat to localhost:2525, logs input and output files, lists spool files and diffs against $OUTPUT, and publish-crates (generates a dynamic pipeline file, artifacts tmp/publish-pipeline.yml after listing crates, checking published versions, and creating test and publish jobs). In the release stage add server-docker-image (extends .kaniko-build-template, runs only on changes to samotop-server/**/*, Dockerfile or .gitlab-ci.yml, sets environment name and URL, variables COMPONENT, CONTEXT, DOCKERFILE, BUILD_ARGS) and publish-crates-trigger (triggers the generated pipeline from publish-crates), plus server-version (produces a dotenv artifact vars.env containing SAMOTOP_VERSION from cargo run -p samotop-server -- --version and SAMOTOP_IMAGE using $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE).
